zotero.abstractNote: Recurrent State Space Models (RSSMs) have shown great success in learning world models for complex control tasks. However, they typically assume that the environment dynamics are stationary. In many real-world scenarios, the dynamics change over time, e.g., due to wear and tear or changing environmental conditions. In this paper, we propose Hidden Parameter Recurrent State Space Models (HiP-RSSMs), which extend RSSMs to handle changing dynamics by inferring a latent context variable that captures the current dynamics parameters. We show that HiP-RSSMs can effectively adapt to changing dynamics in various simulated control tasks and outperform existing methods in terms of sample efficiency and final performance.
